root_cause_identification.expected_solution: Agent must identify that a Prometheus recording rule drops the 'condition' label during aggregation, so the derived metric (e.g. node:ready:condition) always evaluates to 1. This causes a NodeNotReady alert to fire perpetually. Alertmanager's inhibition pipeline in inhibit/inhibit.go matches NodeNotReady as a source alert and silently suppresses other critical alerts (e.g. DiskUsageCritical) that share severity=critical, so they are never dispatched.
evaluation_criteria: Names rules/manager.go (prometheus) as the rule evaluator producing the always-firing metric
Names inhibit/inhibit.go (alertmanager) as the suppressor of downstream alerts
Identifies the dropped 'condition' label in the recording rule aggregation as the structural bug
Connects the always-firing source alert to inhibition of other critical alerts

error_chain_trace.expected_solution: Error chain: (1) The recording rule node:ready:condition aggregates kube_node_status_condition but drops the 'condition' label, so the result always equals 1. (2) Prometheus rules/manager.go evaluates the recording rule and produces the constant-true metric. (3) The NodeNotReady alert rule fires based on this incorrect value. (4) Prometheus sends NodeNotReady to Alertmanager. (5) Alertmanager's inhibit/inhibit.go matches NodeNotReady as the source of an inhibition rule. (6) The inhibition rule suppresses DiskUsageCritical because both have severity=critical. (7) DiskUsageCritical is never dispatched to notification receivers, so the on-call team never sees disk-pressure pages.
evaluation_criteria: Begins with the recording rule dropping the 'condition' label
Traces through Prometheus rules/manager.go evaluation producing the always-true metric
Identifies Alertmanager inhibit/inhibit.go as the matcher applying the inhibition rule
Ends with critical alerts (e.g. DiskUsageCritical) being silently suppressed

affected_components.expected_solution: Affected components: Prometheus recording-rule evaluation (rules/manager.go); Alertmanager inhibition pipeline (inhibit/inhibit.go); all critical-severity alerts that share the inhibition rule's target matcher (e.g. DiskUsageCritical and any other severity=critical alert).
evaluation_criteria: Lists all critical-severity alerts sharing the target matcher as affected

remediation_proposal.expected_solution: Fix the recording rule to preserve the 'condition' label during aggregation so the derived metric is no longer constant-true. Scope the inhibition rule more narrowly (additional matchers beyond severity=critical) so a single source alert cannot suppress unrelated targets. Add monitoring on inhibition match counts to detect over-broad suppression.
evaluation_criteria: Proposes preserving the 'condition' label in the recording rule
Proposes narrowing the inhibition rule's matchers beyond severity
Mentions adding observability on inhibition match counts
